profile.tagline: A man who reveals himself in layers. Choose a world to begin.

heading: Data.
Clarity.
Initiative.
professional.quote: I work at the intersection of data and human understanding, making complex things clear, and finding better ways before anyone asks me to.
professional.bio: I'm Hemanth — a Senior Data Analyst at EY, working at the crossroads of data, technology, and people. My journey started in a computer science classroom in Guntur, building projects with friends that weren't impressive by industry standards but taught me something more valuable — that the best work happens when people build things together. Today I translate complexity into clarity, automate what doesn't need to be manual, and quietly explore where technology is heading next.

bullets: Validate and reconcile client data across multiple enterprise platforms ensuring accuracy and consistency
Execute monthly runs for an internally developed analytics and reporting tool used by client teams
Automate repetitive Excel workflows using Python, reducing manual effort without being asked
Leverage GenAI tools to simplify complex report findings for non-technical end users

projects.name: Attendance Management System
projects.desc: Built with friends during BTech. Automated real-time attendance marking using facial capture, reducing manual intervention entirely. The tech was interesting. Building it together was the point.

projects.name: Emotion Detection & Analysis
projects.stack: Python · Facial Recognition APIs · Emotion Libraries
projects.desc: Designed a model that reads human emotions in real time. Integrated with a drone delivery system to supply items to employees based on emotional state. A small experiment in making technology genuinely care about people.

heading: Warm.
Deep.
Human.
personal.quote: I'm not the loudest person in the room. But if you stay long enough, you'll never want to leave.
personal.bio: I'm Hemanth. From Mangalagiri, Guntur. A small town that taught me big things early. I learned responsibility before I was supposed to. I found joy in the most unexpected places, a friend I met in college who gave me music, a cricket match that felt like a philosophy, a stranger who became someone important within an hour. I reveal myself slowly. But the people who stay find someone intensely loyal, genuinely curious, and powered entirely by the happiness of the people around him.

hobbies.title: Singing
hobbies.note: Not professionally. Not perfectly. Just fully. Music is where I go when words aren't enough.

hobbies.title: Cricket
hobbies.note: Not just a sport, a philosophy. Virat Kohli taught me you can be fierce and still lift others. I try to live that way.

hobbies.title: Pencil Art
hobbies.note: Quiet, patient, detail-oriented. Everything I am when no one is watching.

hobbies.title: Exploring AI
hobbies.note: I spend my own time and honestly, my own money on exploring different LLMs and where this technology is taking us. Not for work. For wonder.

personal.closing: I feel most alive when the people I love are okay.

emotional.heading: A letter.  Unsigned.
paragraphs: I have loved people who didn't stay.
Not romantically, not all of them.
But in the way that matters just as much.

A girl I called sister before the word was official.
A friend who put music in me without knowing it.
People who became homes before I realized
I had moved in completely.

Some of them are gone now.
Not dramatically.
Just quietly.
The way some doors close without slamming.

I heard some of them were hurt.
And even now, in my own silence,
I hope they are okay.

That's the part I can't explain to most people:
how I can miss someone
and still want nothing but good things for them.

I don't know your name yet.
But I've been preparing for you
in ways I didn't even realize.

Every person who left
taught me something about the kind of person
I want to be for the one who stays.

And I intend to stay.
Not because I have to.
Not because leaving is hard.
But because once I am in,
truly in,
leaving stops being a language I speak.

I'm not someone who loves easily.
I'm someone who loves completely.

I won't say it loudly.
I won't perform it for the world.
I'll just be there,
consistently, quietly, entirely.

The kind of there that doesn't disappear when things get heavy.
The kind that remembers the small things
because to me, nothing about you is small.
The kind that shows up,
not sometimes, not when it's convenient,
but always.

I'll be honest with you.
I have overextended myself before.
Held on longer than I should have.
Asked myself "what did I do wrong"
when the real answer was simply this:
some people cannot meet you
at the depth you live in.

I'm still learning where to stop giving.
Still learning how to protect
what I so freely offer.
But I'm not learning to love less.
Never that.

I just want someone who stays.
Not because I tried hard enough.
Not because they had to.
But because they genuinely want to.

Because I am here,
fully, permanently, without condition,
and somewhere out there,
so are you.

I don't know if we've met yet.
I don't know if you're close or still far.
But I know this:
When it's you, I'll know.

And when I know,
there will be no version of my life
that doesn't have you in it.
Yours, before you even knew it.
